one_line_value_proposition: G-Cloud 14 listed remote monitoring platform with dual NICE recommendations (HTE19 + ARI), 25 million global registrations and the UK's largest COPD monitoring programme at NWL ICS.
why_it_matters_locally: Luscii enables ICBs to build virtual COPD wards at scale without lengthy configuration delays — 150+ pre-built NHS pathways are available immediately. NWL ICS enrolled 6,000 COPD patients one year ahead of schedule. G-Cloud 14 listing means no separate OJEU tender is required.
target_problem_statement: Limited virtual ward capacity, high COPD admission rates, post-exacerbation monitoring gaps and slow procurement of remote monitoring technology.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Separate recommendation for Acute Respiratory Infection remote monitoring

evidence_summary: Real-world evidence from NWL ICS (6,000 COPD patients), Bradford District and Craven ICB (6,000 COPD patients, £400k NHSE investment), Sunderland virtual COPD clinic (50% admission reduction — attribution caveated), and MTW NHS Trust virtual ward. Imperial College HFrEF retention study (2% drop-out at 3 months). Published SAFE@HOME academic studies (preeclampsia). DTAC confirmed in G-Cloud 14 (April 2024). No standalone peer-reviewed RCT for Luscii in COPD identified.

case_studies.title: NWL ICS — UK's largest COPD remote monitoring programme
case_studies.setting: North West London ICS / Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust, 2022–2023
case_studies.outcome: 6,000 COPD patient target met within 1 year — 12 months ahead of the 2-year programme target. MyCare24 24/7 monitoring hub operational.
case_studies.caveat: Programme scale and pace data from Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition — not independently peer-reviewed. Outcomes reflect specific NWL ICS configuration including 24/7 hub model.
case_studies.source: Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (NHS Digital Marketplace, 2024)

case_studies.title: Bradford District and Craven ICB — COPD scale-up
case_studies.outcome: 6,000 COPD patients enrolled over 2 years. £400,000+ NHSE investment. Commenced as 50-patient COVID-19 recovery pilot.
case_studies.caveat: Source is Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition — not independently peer-reviewed.
case_studies.source: Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (NHS Digital Marketplace, 2024)

live_sites: NWL ICS / Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust, Bradford District and Craven ICB, South Tyneside and Sunderland NHS FT (attribution caveated), Airedale NHS Foundation Trust, Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells NHS Trust, NHS Dorset (asthma), 11+ countries internationally
patients_covered_note: 25 million+ global registrations across all conditions. 6,000+ COPD patients in NWL ICS alone. 350+ remote monitoring pathways delivered globally.
dtac_note: DTAC passed — confirmed in G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (April 2024). Most recently confirmed DTAC status of the four HTE19-recommended COPD apps.
gdpr_note: Patient data on Amazon AWS servers in Germany. Encrypted at rest. Daily full backups + 5-minute transaction logs. DPIA required as part of local commissioning process.
target_patients: Adults with COPD (any severity). Platform also supports 150+ other LTC pathways.
exclusions: Pulse oximeter required for standard COPD protocol — organisations must procure and supply devices to patients.
onboarding_detail: Introductory patient call model (as per NWL ICS). Patient sets up app and Bluetooth oximeter with clinical support. 150+ pre-built pathways available immediately from Luscii Library. Custom pathways via Luscii Builder (Medical Developer certification required).
supplier_wrap: Co-creates remote monitoring strategy with each NHS organisation. Comprehensive implementation plans and stakeholder mapping. Luscii staff visit wards during setup. 24/7 monitoring hub support available.
local_wraparound_detail: Clinical team or contracted 24/7 monitoring hub required. Pulse oximeters must be procured and supplied to patients. AWS Germany data residency requires DPIA review. Pathway design needed (either pre-built or custom via Builder).
training_note: Certified Medical Developer programme available: clinicians complete online course → certificate → Luscii Builder access. 70% revenue share for Library-listed programmes.
monitoring_note: Active remote management model. Clinical Engine AI generates alerts for missed measurements, out-of-range readings and patient comments. Must be reviewed by clinical team or contracted hub.
escalation_note: Alerts projected as FHIR Flags to connected EHR/EPR. Clinical team configures alert thresholds and escalation protocols.
operating_hours_caveat: Virtual ward model requires staffed monitoring capability — either in-house or contracted (e.g. MyCare24 model). Confirm monitoring hours before deployment.
implementation_prerequisites: Pulse oximeters procured and supplied to patients
Clinical team or 24/7 monitoring hub contracted
DPIA completed for AWS Germany data residency
Pathway selected from Library or built via Luscii Builder
indicative_price_text: Not publicly disclosed. Available via G-Cloud 14 on NHS Digital Marketplace — no separate OJEU tender required. Contact Luscii via luscii.co.uk.
service_wrap_note: Comprehensive implementation support included. Confirm scope of 24/7 monitoring hub support in contract.
contract_note: Listed on NHS G-Cloud 14 (Digital Marketplace) as 'System-wide Remote Patient Monitoring Service' — procurement without separate OJEU tender is a significant process advantage.
expected_benefit_note: NWL ICS enrolled 6,000 COPD patients ahead of schedule. Bradford ICB reached 6,000 patients with NHSE investment. Evidence on admission reduction pending peer-reviewed publication.
tariff_considerations: No live tariff calculator available. NWL ICS programme scale suggests potential for significant bed-day savings at population level, but formal peer-reviewed cost model for COPD not identified. Model using local COPD prevalence and hospital admission costs.
provider_income_note: Not stated. Confirm locally whether virtual ward activity affects provider payment arrangements.
roi_note: Sunderland: 50% respiratory admission reduction cited (attribution caveated — see data quality note). NWL: 6,000 patients enrolled 12 months early. Financial ROI model pending NICE HTE19 evidence generation.
nhse_125k_note: Eligibility for NHSE ICB £125k COPD funding not confirmed for Luscii in reviewed sources. Verify with NHSE before including in business case.
data_quality_note: The 50% respiratory admission reduction cited for Sunderland is also claimed by Clinitouch/Spirit Health for the same geography. Attribution cannot be confirmed from public sources — do not use in comparative commissioning claims without verification from South Tyneside and Sunderland NHS Foundation Trust.
demo_notes: Contact Luscii via luscii.co.uk for a configured demonstration environment.
source_summary: NICE HTG736, Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (NHS Digital Marketplace 2024), HTN interview February 2024 (MTW case study), All Together Better Alliance press release March 2020, luscii.co.uk/evidence.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information including G-Cloud 14 Service Definition. Device classification caveated — confirm COPD-specific CE certificate with Luscii. Sunderland attribution unresolved.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: NWL ICS / Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust COPD programme
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Dr Sarah Elkin, Consultant Respiratory Medicine, Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: North West London ICS, 2022–2023. MyCare24 24/7 monitoring hub.
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 6,000 COPD patient target met within 1 year — 12 months ahead of the 2-year programme target. Weekly SpO2/HR monitoring, CAT/CCQ questionnaires, Know Your Normal, and educational lessons deployed.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Data from Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition — not independently peer-reviewed.
clinical_evidence_detailed.source_label: Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (NHS Digital Marketplace, 2024)

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Bradford District and Craven ICB COPD programme
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Dr Katherine Hickman, GP Respiratory Lead, Bradford District and Craven ICB
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Bradford District and Craven ICB, 2021–2023. £400,000+ NHSE investment.
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 6,000 COPD patients enrolled over 2 years. Commenced as 50-patient COVID-19 recovery pilot (2021) and expanded into full COPD programme. Air quality alerts and Know Your Normal in use.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Source is Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition — not independently peer-reviewed.
clinical_evidence_detailed.source_label: Luscii G-Cloud 14 Service Definition (NHS Digital Marketplace, 2024)

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Sunderland — All Together Better Alliance virtual COPD clinic
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Recovery at Home team, South Tyneside and Sunderland NHS FT
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: South Tyneside and Sunderland NHS FT, pilot commenced March 2020
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 50% reduction in respiratory emergency admissions. 24/7 virtual COPD clinic. Socioeconomically deprived population; iPads provided to address digital inclusion.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: All Together Better press release (2020) confirms Luscii was the pilot technology. However, later Clinitouch deployment in same area creates ambiguity about longer-term outcomes data.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: Attribution unverified. Same outcome data also cited by Clinitouch/Spirit Health for the same geography. Verify with South Tyneside and Sunderland NHS FT before using in comparative commissioning materials.
clinical_evidence_detailed.source_label: All Together Better Alliance press release (March 2020)

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells NHS Trust virtual COPD ward
clinical_evidence_detailed.authors: Respiratory team, Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells NHS Trust
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells NHS Trust, 2023–2024
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Virtual COPD ward expanded from monitoring-only to include acute and A&E escalation pathways. Luscii Builder used to create bespoke escalation pathway.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: Imperial College — Heart Failure retention study
clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Imperial College Healthcare NHS Trust, HFrEF patients, 3 months
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: 2% drop-out rate at 3 months. Demonstrates strong patient retention in Luscii-enabled monitoring pathways.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Specific peer-reviewed publication not identified in reviewed sources. Source is Luscii UK evidence page.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Published peer-reviewed studies on Luscii in preeclampsia remote monitoring. Demonstrates platform evidence generation capability across conditions beyond COPD.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Not directly applicable to COPD commissioning decisions. Cross-condition platform evidence only.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Recommended for NHS use during 3-year evidence generation period. DTAC confirmed (G-Cloud 14 April 2024).
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Evidence generation period — full NICE recommendation pending ~December 2027.

monitoring_model: Active remote monitoring with automated triage and clinical escalation pathways.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Dedicated monitoring hub or virtual ward team, OMRON devices distributed to patients, IT infrastructure for device connectivity.
procurement_notes: Available via SBRI Healthcare contract. Direct procurement with Luscii/OMRON also possible.
cyber_notes: Netherlands-based company — confirm data residency and DPA terms for NHS use. ISO 27001 certified.

technical_integrations.device_integration: OMRON pulse oximeters, blood pressure monitors, spirometers via Bluetooth

context_of_use.population: Adults with moderate-severe COPD or post-hospitalisation COPD patients
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Active remote monitoring, automated triage, early exacerbation detection, population health management
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Active — monitoring hub team required for triage and escalation
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTE19 (HTG736) — recommended during 3-year evidence generation period
